title: Sarepta Therapeutics Q1 2024 Earnings Call Summary - Strong Revenue Growth and Gene Therapy Progress
text:
Sarepta reported strong Q1 2024 results with total revenue of $413.5 million, up significantly from $253.5 million in Q1 2023. Net product revenue reached approximately $360 million, representing over 55% growth year-over-year.

ELEVIDYS (Gene Therapy) net product revenue: $133.9 million. The company noted that sales have plateaued as anticipated while awaiting label expansion approval. Over $334 million in cumulative sales since June 2023 approval.

PMO Franchise (RNA Therapies) combined net product revenue: $225.5 million. EXONDYS 51: $120.2 million, AMONDYS 45: $71.9 million, VYONDYS 53: $33.5 million. Revenue remained relatively flat compared to Q1 2023.

Regulatory Update: Management indicated they are in a quiet period regarding the ELEVIDYS BLA supplement, which seeks to remove age and ambulation restrictions. The company stated they will be receiving their draft ELEVIDYS label very shortly with an official action date of June 21.

R&D Progress: EMERGENE trial (SRP-9003 for LGMD2E) enrollment has begun. Real-world evidence published showing eteplirsen users survived 5.4 years longer than untreated patients. SRP-5051 peptide-conjugated PMO showed strong dystrophin production results.

Financial Position: The company maintains approximately $1.4 billion in cash and investments, positioning them to support potential label expansion and ongoing clinical development.
